name: Architext 1.7.1 Serve Startup Fix
summary: Patch release: architext serve binds and opens its URL immediately instead of blocking on the full plan-farm precompute, so a large corpus no longer makes the CLI appear frozen during startup.
updateSource: Shipped to npm 2026-06-20. All six packages published at 1.7.1 and verified live (npm install -g @robotaccomplice/architext@1.7.1 resolves the platform binary; architext --version -> 1.7.1). Ships the serve non-blocking farm-warm fix (PR #225): time-to-reachable on a 15-flow x 30-view corpus 96,316 ms -> 91 ms.

required.title: Non-blocking serve startup (background farm warm)
required.summary: The CLI serve path called build_farm(), which synchronously precomputes every flow x view plan before binding the listener and printing the URL, so `architext serve` on a large corpus sat silently for ~96s and looked frozen. Build an empty farm so the server binds and prints its URL immediately, and warm the farm in a background spawn_blocking task; the viewer polls the farm and shows its progress overlay while diagrams compute.

workstreams.name: Patch corrections
workstreams.summary: Serve startup latency fix shipped as a patch on top of 1.7.0.

evidence.label: PR #225 serve: bind immediately, warm the plan farm in the background
